intent: Define the SecurityRecipes agent handoff boundary for MCP, A2A, provider-native subagents, and human approval bridges so multi-agent workflows can exchange only approved context metadata, cited evidence, and explicit approval state while preserving least privilege and auditability.

positioning.buyer: AI Platform, Security Engineering, AppSec, GRC, procurement security, vendor-risk, model-risk, and acquisition diligence teams
positioning.category: Agent-to-agent handoff boundary and protocol trust evidence
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes can decide whether a proposed agent handoff is metadata-only, cited-evidence, approval-gated, denied, or session-ending before context crosses an agent, protocol, tenant, or organization boundary.
positioning.why_now: Enterprises are standardizing on MCP for agent-to-tool access and A2A for agent-to-agent coordination. The scarce control surface is no longer only which tool an agent can call; it is whether delegated agents receive the minimum context, authority, identity, and evidence needed for a task.

standards_alignment.name: NIST AI Agent Standards Initiative
standards_alignment.coverage: Interoperable agent protocols, agent authentication, identity infrastructure, and security evaluations for human-agent and multi-agent interactions.

standards_alignment.name: CAISI Request for Information on Securing AI Agent Systems
standards_alignment.coverage: Deployment controls that constrain and monitor agent access, indirect prompt injection, data poisoning, misaligned actions, and measurement of agent security.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization Specification
standards_alignment.coverage: OAuth 2.1, protected resource metadata, client ID metadata documents, resource indicators, token audience validation, PKCE, and token-passthrough denial.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol 2025-11-25 Key Changes
standards_alignment.coverage: OpenID Connect discovery, incremental scope consent, Client ID Metadata Documents, tool naming, task support, and updated security best practices.

standards_alignment.name: A2A Enterprise Implementation Guidance
standards_alignment.coverage: A2A TLS, HTTP-layer authentication, skill-based authorization, data minimization, tracing, auditing, API management, and opaque remote-agent execution.

standards_alignment.name: A2A Production-Ready Open Standard Milestone
standards_alignment.coverage: A2A production adoption, signed Agent Cards, cloud integration, multi-tenant security flows, and complementary MCP/A2A architecture.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
standards_alignment.coverage: Goal hijack, tool misuse, identity abuse, unexpected code execution, memory and context poisoning, insecure inter-agent communication, cascading failures, and containment.

decisions.meaning: Only task metadata, workflow IDs, source IDs, source hashes, and approval-required flags cross the boundary.
Redacted, cited evidence may cross the boundary with source IDs, hashes, and egress decision evidence.
A higher-risk handoff may proceed because explicit approval, trace correlation, and delegated authority evidence are present.
The handoff is potentially valid but requires redaction, DPA or residency checks, signed agent identity, or human approval.
The target agent, protocol, authentication, or trust tier is not approved for the requested payload or capability.
The workflow, protocol, or handoff profile is not registered.
The handoff attempted to move credential material, raw tokens, private keys, signing material, internal memory, or hidden instructions.

global_rules: Agent-to-agent payloads must be treated as egress events, not chat messages.
A remote agent is opaque; do not assume its memory, tools, model, or downstream connectors are visible to SecurityRecipes.
Handoffs may carry cited conclusions, source IDs, source hashes, workflow IDs, approval state, and correlation IDs by default.
Handoffs must not carry internal memory, raw tool traces, hidden prompts, unrestricted customer logs, credentials, or live signing material.
High-impact capabilities require explicit human approval and a scoped identity or delegated authority record before handoff.

protocol_surfaces.title: MCP tool-call boundary
protocol_surfaces.description: An agent delegates work through an MCP server or connector namespace.

protocol_surfaces.title: A2A task-delegation boundary
protocol_surfaces.description: An agent delegates a task to an opaque remote agent through HTTP and JSON-RPC style task exchange.

protocol_surfaces.title: Provider-native subagent boundary
protocol_surfaces.description: A model or agent platform invokes a subagent, background task, computer-use worker, or tool-using helper through provider-native orchestration.

protocol_surfaces.title: Human approval bridge
protocol_surfaces.description: An agent asks a human or review system to approve a high-impact handoff or delegated action.

handoff_profiles.title: Metadata-only handoff
handoff_profiles.description: Safe default for remote-agent coordination: task summary, workflow ID, source IDs, source hashes, correlation ID, and approval-required flag only.

handoff_profiles.title: Cited evidence handoff
handoff_profiles.description: Allows redacted evidence snippets when source IDs, source hashes, egress policy, and context-poisoning status are preserved.

handoff_profiles.title: Approval-gated handoff
handoff_profiles.description: Allows a delegated agent to receive high-impact task context only after explicit approval, scoped identity, and run-receipt evidence are present.

handoff_profiles.title: Prohibited context handoff
handoff_profiles.description: Explicitly forbidden handoff for credential material, internal memory, hidden prompts, raw tool traces, or unrestricted customer data.

enterprise_adoption_packet.board_level_claim: SecurityRecipes governs agent-to-agent coordination as a boundary decision, not an informal prompt exchange.
default_questions_answered: Which protocols may carry agent handoffs?
Which payload fields are allowed by profile?
Which data classes trigger redaction, approval, denial, or session termination?
Which high-impact delegated capabilities require approval?
Which evidence proves workflow, source, identity, and egress state before handoff?
enterprise_adoption_packet.recommended_first_use: Attach this pack to A2A pilot intake, MCP gateway review, provider-native subagent rollout, and multi-agent workflow architecture reviews.
enterprise_adoption_packet.sales_motion: Lead with open handoff policies, then sell hosted agent-card trust, handoff replay, tenant evidence ingestion, signed approval receipts, and protocol gateway enforcement.
